name: Salina Interparochial
description_short: Salina Interparochial Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving the Salina, Kansas area with credit union banking services since at least 2022.
description_long:
Salina Interparochial Credit Union operates as a credit union—a member-owned financial institution—based in Salina, Kansas at 1927 S. Ohio St. The organization is listed as a member of the Salina Area Chamber of Commerce since 2022, indicating it is an established local financial services provider.

As a credit union, Salina Interparochial likely offers standard banking and financial services typical of credit unions, including member savings accounts, checking accounts, loans, and other financial products. Credit unions are cooperative financial institutions where members are both customers and partial owners, theoretically allowing for more favorable terms and member-focused services compared to traditional banks.

The limited information available on the website suggests Salina Interparochial positions itself as a community-oriented financial institution. Its membership in the local Chamber of Commerce indicates engagement with the Salina business community and commitment to local economic participation.

A significant limitation is that the available website content does not provide detailed information about specific products, interest rates, fees, lending criteria, membership requirements, or service offerings. Prospective members would need to contact the institution directly at 785-825-0744 to understand the full range of services, eligibility requirements, and competitive terms compared to other financial institutions in the area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers more favorable terms than traditional banks
Local community presence in Salina with established Chamber of Commerce membership since 2022
Direct contact available via phone (785-825-0744) for member inquiries
Physical branch location at 1927 S. Ohio St., Salina, KS for in-person banking
Credit union model emphasizes member benefits and financial cooperation
cons: Website provides minimal information about specific products, services, rates, or fees
No online service offerings, account details, or membership requirements listed
Limited transparency about lending criteria, loan amounts, or membership eligibility
No information about insurance products, investment services, or advanced financial tools
Potential membership restrictions common to credit unions not disclosed on available materials
best_for: Salina-area residents seeking local, member-owned banking alternatives
Individuals interested in credit union membership benefits and cooperative finance
Community members prioritizing local financial institution support
diagnosis: Salina Interparochial Credit Union is best suited for Salina-area residents seeking local, member-owned banking services with a cooperative structure. The primary caveat is that the public website provides almost no detail about specific products, rates, fees, or membership requirements—prospective members must contact the institution directly for substantive information about services and eligibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
